behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A pure in-process mock could easily return a pre-filtered diff and hide real scoping bugs. REAL-INFRA: The failure manifests when extracting changed lines from GitHub's actual unified diff format, which includes context lines. An Integration test with a realistic mock GitHub response (including context lines with proper +/- prefixes) can catch the extraction logic bug without requiring a real PR. A Unit test of diff parsing could work but would likely mock the diff structure too abstractly.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API to return a realistic unified diff that includes context lines (unchanged lines) alongside actual changes. Run the agent on this diff. Verify that the analysis engine receives only lines marked with +/- (changed lines) in its input, not the context lines. Test across multiple files with varying ratios of context to changes to ensure the filtering is correct.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: A mock GitHub API will accept any position value, always passing and hiding real position validation bugs. REAL-INFRA: GitHub has specific position numbering for diffs and rejects invalid positions with a 422 error. This behavior is entirely GitHub-specific. An Integration test with a mock cannot catch the failure because the mock would not enforce GitHub's position constraints. Only the real GitHub API validates that a position exists within the actual diff bounds.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real GitHub pull request with a known code change. Have the agent post inline review comments at specific line numbers in the diff view. Verify that each comment appears at the correct position in GitHub's actual UI. Test edge cases: comments at the start, middle, and end of the diff, and comments across multiple files. Real GitHub API validation is required to catch position numbering errors.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: Correctness depends entirely on what the real LLM produces. The agent's ability to detect bugs is a capability of the LLM itself. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the LLM to always return 'bug found' tests your mock's response logic, not the system's actual defect detection capability. The real failure is when the LLM fails to identify a subtle bug. This only happens with the real LLM API because LLMs are non-deterministic — the same code snippet may produce different analysis results across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Provide the real LLM API (not a mock) with a code snippet from a PR that contains a known, realistic bug (e.g., race condition, missing null check, uninitialized variable). Instruct the agent to analyze and identify defects. Verify that the agent's findings include the known bug. Run the test 5+ times with the real LLM to account for non-determinism. The test should use a code defect that is subtle but discoverable by a capable LLM, not a trivial or obvious bug.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is a logical consistency check, not a GitHub-specific behavior. A mock can easily capture all outgoing requests (inline comments and summary comment). MOCK-HIDE: No — a mock would not hide this failure; it would expose inconsistencies in the request sequence. This is an internal consistency check within the agent's own output paths. If the agent posts 5 findings as inline comments but only mentions 3 in the summary, an Integration test that captures all requests will catch this mismatch.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API. Run the agent on a test PR with identifiable findings. Intercept and log all POST requests (both inline comment and summary comment payloads). Parse the findings mentioned in the summary comment. For each finding in the summary, verify that a matching inline comment was posted in the intercepted requests. Verify that no additional findings appear in the summary that lack a corresponding inline comment. This tests the agent's internal consistency without requiring real GitHub.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: The requirement explicitly mentions 'real pull request,' indicating a real GitHub PR. The full sequence is: (1) read diff from GitHub, (2) call LLM to identify bugs, (3) post inline comments, (4) post summary. Step 2 is agentic (depends on real LLM output). LLM-DEP: If the agent's bug detection is part of the 'full sequence,' it requires a real LLM. REAL-INFRA: 'Real pull request' requires real GitHub infrastructure. An Integration test (mocked LLM + mocked GitHub) would miss real GitHub API issues and LLM output variability. A System test (real GitHub + mocked LLM) would miss LLM-dependent bugs. An Agentic test (real LLM + mocked GitHub) would miss GitHub integration issues. Only Workflow (real LLM + real GitHub) tests the complete pipeline as it would run in production.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real GitHub pull request with code containing a realistic defect. Run the entire agent pipeline end-to-end using real GitHub API calls and real LLM API calls: (1) read the PR diff, (2) call the real LLM to identify bugs, (3) post inline review comments for each finding, (4) post a top-level summary comment. Verify that all steps complete without error, inline comments are posted before the summary, the sequence completes without manual intervention, and the findings in the summary match the inline comments posted. Log timing to verify correct sequencing. The test validates the full production workflow.
